mentions.note: Should have terrific quarter given volatility; waiting for company to attract serious institutional investors beyond traders.
Caller bought at $129, down to $70; sells at 28x earnings vs. Morgan Stanley at 14x; less sticky capital than traditional brokers; room to go down even though good company.
Caller Cheryl caught at high prices; focus on future, not past entry—would buy at $80, very big at $70; forget where you bought, ask where it can go.
Retail brokerage surged as risk appetite returns; pure sentiment play on bullish animal spirits unleashed by geopolitical improvement.

Retail trading platform; miserable Q4 (soft options/crypto trading); −24% Feb, −17% two-session decline. Cramer prefers SoFi for youth fintech, but turning bullish on crypto may support near-term rebound.
Trading 30x earnings but portfolio skewed to crypto; Cramer wants more equity/bond mix before owning; too volatile/Bitcoin-correlated as-is.
Closely aligned with Bitcoin price; Bitcoin lost luster; precipitous decline challenges crypto as store of value; may be repository of something toxic.
Captured young investor cohort; strong product engagement and user loyalty; durable positioning with younger demographic.
Up 200% in 2025 (fifth-best S&P performer); pulled back 20% from Oct peak on crypto/spec weakness; long-term story strong as younger generation learns investing, inherits $100T+.
